name: cf-access-proxy
description: Local HTTP proxy that adds CF-Access-Client-Id / -Secret headers to upstream requests. Lets VS Code chat extensions (which don't support custom headers) reach a Cloudflare-Access-gated OpenAI-compatible endpoint via http://127.0.0.1:<port>.

cfAccessProxy.host.description: Upstream host (e.g. api.nextgarch.org). The proxy forwards every request to https://<host>:443/<original-path>.

cfAccessProxy.port.description: Local TCP port the proxy listens on. Point your chat extension's OpenAI base URL at http://127.0.0.1:<this-port>.

cfAccessProxy.clientId.description: CF Access service-token client ID (the value ending in .access from your Cloudflare Zero Trust service token). The matching secret is stored separately via the "CF Access Proxy: Set Client Secret" command, never in settings.json.

cfAccessProxy.enable.description: Start the proxy when VS Code launches. Disable to keep the extension installed but stop the listener.

cfAccessProxy.disableThinking.description: Inject thinking-disable fields into POST /v1/chat/completions bodies that don't set them. Adds both chat_template_kwargs.enable_thinking=false (vLLM-style, used by Qwen3, DeepSeek-V4/R1) and top-level think=false (Ollama-style, 0.3.x+). Each backend ignores the field it doesn't know. Makes reasoning models emit OpenAI-spec-compliant streams (content only, no reasoning_content) — fixes 'no response was returned' in clients like Copilot Chat. Set false to pass through reasoning unchanged.

cfAccessProxy.stripEmptyToolCalls.description: Strip empty tool_calls:[] fields from /v1/chat/completions response chunks (SSE delta and non-stream message). vLLM emits an empty array alongside content when the request includes tools, which strict OpenAI clients (Copilot Chat) interpret as a tool-call message — causing them to ignore the content. Real (non-empty) tool calls pass through untouched.

commands.title: CF Access Proxy: Set Client Secret
CF Access Proxy: Clear Client Secret
